Class GotoNextSiblingInstruction

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.rules.matcher.vm.instruction.GotoNextSiblingInstruction
All Implemented Interfaces:
VMInstruction
public final class GotoNextSiblingInstruction extends Object implements VMInstruction
A VMInstruction that moves the PoolSyntaxElementCursor to the next sibling of the current syntax element.

This instruction is used to traverse horizontally within the syntax tree structure— typically from one argument or subcomponent of a compound term to the next. It does not perform any pattern matching itself and always returns the current MatchResultInfo unchanged.

Useful in instruction sequences that iterate over multiple components of a node.

  • Method Details

    • match

      public MatchResultInfo match(PoolSyntaxElementCursor cursor, MatchResultInfo matchConditions, LogicServices services)
      Moves the cursor to the next sibling of the current syntax element.
      Specified by:
      match in interface VMInstruction
      Parameters:
      cursor - the navigation cursor for traversing the syntax structure
      matchConditions - the current matching state, returned unchanged
      services - logic services (not used in this instruction)
      Returns:
      the unchanged matchConditions
